purpose: Reusable policy for choosing institutional identity routes before browser-backed publisher PDF downloads.

subscription_institution.notes: Ask for the user's own subscription institution only when no institution is configured. Save the most recent explicit institution choice in local config.carsi_idp_name, with optional English and Chinese/local aliases for page matching. Do not default to Example University or any other school.

routing_rules: Ask the user for their own subscription institution when no institution is configured; after an explicit choice, save it in local config and reuse it on later runs.
For off-campus access to publishers that support Shibboleth or OpenAthens, prefer federated institutional authentication over WebVPN.
When a publisher/institution-specific WAYFless link is configured, use it to skip group/institution selection and enter the institution IdP directly.
If the WAYFless link fails, fall back to the publisher's standard Shibboleth/OpenAthens institution-selection flow.
Do not make WebVPN a global default because not every institution provides a WebVPN gateway.
Use WebVPN only when the configured institution has a WebVPN-style gateway and the publisher route has been browser-verified through that gateway.
Do not treat exported cookies as full browser session persistence. Cookie stores are useful for HTTP preflight and some request reuse only.
For closed-access publisher PDFs, keep the visible CloakBrowser context alive when SSO, CAPTCHA, 2FA, Cloudflare, or publisher-generated PDF tokens are involved.
A final claim that a publisher can or cannot provide a PDF must come from a visible CloakBrowser workflow, not requests, curl, or cookie replay alone.
When WebVPN fails to provide a PDF, fall back to the publisher-specific broker and article-page institutional login flow before marking the publisher failed.

publisher_broker.persistence_notes: The same visible CloakBrowser context preserves browser storage, challenge state, page-generated PDF tokens, and session cookies. The persistent profile is the disk-backed fallback when the broker is restarted.

cookie_store.notes: Saved cookies can validate simple gateway reachability, but they do not preserve all browser storage, WebVPN in-memory state, TLS session tickets, publisher challenge state, or generated PDF tokens.

browser_profile_dir.notes: A persistent browser profile can keep cookies, localStorage, IndexedDB, service workers, and cache. It still may not restore live connection state, TLS sessions, or challenge state after the process exits.

webvpn.final_verdict_scope: browser verified only when the PDF is captured in the active WebVPN CloakBrowser context

wayfless_federated_sso.notes: Use institution-specific WAYFless links when configured, because they skip the federation group and institution picker and go directly to the institution IdP.

standard_federated_sso.notes: Use publisher controls such as Sign in by institution, Log in via Shibboleth, OpenAthens, CARSI, China CERNET Federation, or institution search.

federated_sso.persistence_notes: Use article-page controls such as Institutional Sign In, OpenAthens, Access through your institution, or Access through your organization. The user completes credentials, 2FA, and CAPTCHA manually.

campus_connector.persistence_notes: Some schools require a local connector instead of WebVPN. InstSci should not assume a WebVPN URL exists for these institutions.

generalizable_rules: Many databases support Shibboleth or OpenAthens institutional authentication.

Standard login flow: publisher login page -> institutional/Shibboleth/OpenAthens login -> federation group such as China CERNET Federation or CARSI when shown -> user's own institution -> institution IdP.
WAYFless links can skip federation and institution selection when the institution-specific link is configured.
If a WAYFless shortcut fails, switch to the standard Shibboleth/OpenAthens login page.
These rules should be parameterized by the user's subscription institution; Example University is not a default.

Example University_webvpn_2026_06_07.summary: The WebVPN login itself succeeded, but saved gateway cookies did not restore the full browser WebVPN session in a new context, and the same live WebVPN CloakBrowser context did not provide direct cross-publisher PDF capture for the sampled publishers.
